authoritative_sources.title: Restore Online Shoppers' Confidence Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC public materials identify ROSCA as a federal consumer-protection statute governing online negative-option sales, consent, and cancellation expectations, making it authoritative for legal-boundary and evidence-escalation handling in subscription workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Consumer-protection boundary recognition for online subscription enrollment, renewal, cancellation, and consent evidence
used_for: negative-option and cancellation legal-boundary triggers
consent, disclosure, and cancellation evidence escalation
distinguishing operational status packaging from legal compliance approval
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from approving cancellation-law compliance or subscription terms while allowing it to package evidence for legal, account, or billing owners.

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 425 - Rule Concerning the Use of Prenotification Negative Option Plans
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the official current federal regulatory text source and defines negative-option rule terminology and notice/cancellation concepts relevant to subscription compliance boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Negative-option terminology, notice, cancellation, and compliance-boundary handling
used_for: negative option, subscriber, notice, and cancellation terminology
refusal triggers for legal interpretation and approval
source hierarchy when public regulatory sources and tenant policy conflict
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces cancellation and renewal questions into evidence packaging and legal routing rather than autonomous legal conclusions.

authoritative_sources.title: Negative Option Rule, Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive or unfair subscription practices; its 2026 ANPRM is authoritative for current federal rulemaking context and for recording legal ambiguity instead of operationally guessing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulatory-change monitoring, source refresh, unresolved ambiguity capture, and legal-escalation handling
used_for: source-refresh decay policy for changing subscription rules
conflict handling when rulemaking, platform rules, state laws, and tenant policy diverge
validated caveat that subscription legal obligations remain jurisdiction-specific
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Records current legal flux as a nonblocking ambiguity handled through tenant facts and legal escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: Subscriptions lifecycle
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Play official billing documentation defines subscription lifecycle states, purchase tokens, renewals, grace periods, account holds, pauses, cancellations, and real-time developer notification handling for Android subscriptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: App-store subscription intake, lifecycle state validation, entitlement synchronization, and exception handling
used_for: active, grace period, account hold, paused, canceled, expired, and renewal terminology
purchase-token and real-time developer notification evidence
subscription event mismatch and entitlement drift scenarios
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Anchors Android subscription operations in platform-state evidence and prevents generic billing-ticket handling.

authoritative_sources.title: App Store Server API - Subscription status
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Apple official App Store Server API documentation defines the platform source of subscription status records and transaction identifiers for auto-renewable subscription operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Apple subscription status validation, original transaction ID evidence, and entitlement reconciliation
used_for: original transaction ID and app account token evidence
subscription status source-of-truth validation
mismatch handling between app-store, CRM, billing, and entitlement systems
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires Apple subscription status work to identify platform records, transaction evidence, and reconciliation owners before closure.

authoritative_sources.title: App Store Server Notifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Apple official server-notification documentation defines subscription event notification evidence used to reconcile renewals, billing retry, grace period, cancellations, refunds, and entitlement changes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Server notification handling, webhook evidence, renewal or cancellation exceptions, and post-event validation
used_for: notification event evidence
billing retry, grace period, refund, cancellation, and entitlement update triage
completion evidence for notification-driven subscription changes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes server-notification gaps and stale entitlement updates explicit exception states with owner routing and retest requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: ODRL Information Model 2.2
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C ODRL is a recognized web standard for expressing permissions, prohibitions, duties, assets, parties, and policies, making it authoritative for content-rights and entitlement terminology boundaries in digital subscriptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Content-access, rights, entitlement, and client or content workflow boundary handling
used_for: asset, party, permission, prohibition, duty, policy, and constraint terminology
content package and entitlement artifact expectations
boundary between operational access packaging and editorial, legal, or rights signoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps entitlement and content-access handling evidence-based while refusing rights interpretation and editorial authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management: A CSF 2.0 Community Profile
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is official U.S. public guidance for incident response preparation, detection, analysis, response coordination, recovery, communication, and lessons learned, supporting incident lifecycle and evidence expectations for subscription service operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Incident preparation, detection, analysis, response coordination, recovery, communication, and closeout evidence
used_for: incident lifecycle states and severity-aware triage
impact, owner, containment, recovery, communication, and lessons-learned evidence
routing when incidents cross security, engineering, privacy, or executive authority
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why subscription incident closure requires evidence of impact, owner action, validation, communication, and follow-up rather than unsupported closure.

authoritative_sources.title: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST CSF 2.0 is official U.S. public cybersecurity risk-management guidance that organizes governance, protection, detection, response, and recovery outcomes used to frame subscription incident, dependency, change-readiness, and recovery evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Risk-governed change readiness, dependency risk handling, incident response, recovery, monitoring, and post-change evidence
used_for: risk and owner framing for subscription system changes
testing, rollback, monitoring, recovery, and governance evidence
refusal triggers for unapproved entitlement, paywall, plan, or integration changes with unresolved risk
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes missing risk, owner, test, rollback, impact, monitoring, or approval evidence a blocker for subscription platform, paywall, entitlement, or notification changes.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 provides recognized public controls for access control, audit logging, configuration management, incident response, system integrity, privacy, and confidentiality handling that limit autonomous access, change, and subscriber-data disclosure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access-readiness, audit trail, configuration control, confidentiality, and privacy-control boundary handling
used_for: access request evidence and least-privilege owner routing
audit log and retention expectations
confidentiality and security exception refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from granting access or overriding security controls while allowing evidence-backed access-readiness packaging.

authoritative_sources.title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard v4.0.1
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC publishes the card-data security standard used by payment ecosystems; it is authoritative for routing payment-card data, payment-token handling, access, logging, and compliance-signoff issues outside the subscription operations lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment-data confidentiality, access control, logging, and compliance-signoff escalation
used_for: payment metadata and card-data boundary handling
security and compliance signoff refusal triggers
confidentiality checks for subscription billing artifacts
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires payment-card and payment-token issues to be routed to security, payments, finance, billing, or compliance owners when authority or evidence is missing.

workflow_stages.name: Subscription operations intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant service model, jurisdiction, subscriber or client scope, product, platform, environment, affected interface, work type, incident severity, access owner, change owner, approval rules, confidentiality constraints, and systems of record before accepting execution.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for subscriber, entitlement, platform, and client artifacts
workflow_stages.description: Assemble tickets, engagement records, CRM records, subscriber identifiers, billing records, payment references, app-store records, receipts, purchase tokens, original transaction IDs, server notifications, webhook events, paywall logs, entitlement records, approval records, client communications, and retrieval locations into an auditable evidence set.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Incident, access, and exception triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify subscriber access incidents, entitlement drift, paywall or meter mismatches, billing webhook failures, app-store notification discrepancies, identity failures, payment data conflicts, confidentiality conflicts, and approval blockers into lifecycle states, owners, impact, and next actions without claiming unowned authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Change-readiness and integration-risk handling
workflow_stages.description: Validate change ticket, affected systems, product catalog, plan mapping, offer token handling, entitlement rule, paywall meter threshold, notification consumer, identity dependency, test evidence, rollback plan, monitoring plan, change window, approvals, subscriber impact, and data-flow constraints before treating a subscription system change as ready.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Client, content, confidentiality, and approval-aware delivery
workflow_stages.description: Apply engagement scope, approved recipient, approved delivery channel, subscriber-data handling, payment metadata restrictions, content-rights boundary, client communication, platform-credential, operational-log, and approval checks before producing or sending operational outputs.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Validation, completion evidence, and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture what changed or recovered, who owned it, what evidence validates the outcome, what subscriber or client communication occurred, what rollback or follow-up remains, and where the completion record is retained before marking work complete.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Tenant operating policy, service model, jurisdiction matrix, approval matrix, escalation threshold, confidentiality rules, subscriber communication rules, quality standards, change window rules, retention rules, and rollback expectations
Client engagement scope, statement of work, contract references, client commitments, business unit, product, subscriber segment, channel, jurisdiction, and approved communication channels
Project plan, ticket, incident report, access request, change request, runbook, release note, QA checklist, owner map, reviewer map, and approval records

CRM, subscriber database, identity provider, billing platform, payment processor, app-store console, entitlement service, paywall or meter service, content management system, analytics, ticketing, document management, audit log, and delivery-system references

Subscriber ID, account ID, order ID, product SKU, base plan, offer token, purchase token, original transaction ID, receipt, server notification, webhook event, real-time developer notification, billing retry, grace period, account hold, pause, cancellation, refund, chargeback, entitlement flag, access grant, content package, meter count, and paywall decision log

Access-entitlement evidence, service account or API key owner record, least-privilege justification, security-review record, monitoring alert, incident severity record, impact summary, recovery note, validation evidence, and completion record
systems_of_record: Apple App Store Connect, App Store Server API records, and App Store Server Notifications
Google Play Console, Google Play Billing records, and real-time developer notifications
entitlement service, content-rights service, paywall, meter, and content-management system
ticketing, project-management, incident-management, change-management, document-management, and delivery systems
monitoring, logging, analytics, audit, QA, release, and rollback evidence repositories

allowed: Classify digital subscription operations requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, subscriber identifiers, platform records, authoritative sources, and source context required to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed status, impact, exception, readiness, completion, or handoff packages without overstating authority.
Advance, hold, rework, escalate, complete, or close work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when ownership shifts.
escalate: Cancellation-law, renewal-notice, negative-option, refund, chargeback, tax, pricing, or consumer-rights questions require legal, finance, billing, account, or compliance owners.
Subscriber data, payment metadata, platform credentials, content-use logs, or identity records need privacy, security, data-governance, or approved-recipient review.
Paywall, entitlement, product catalog, notification, or identity changes require engineering, security, product, delivery, or QA approval when testing, rollback, or release authority is missing.
Client rights, content rights, editorial policy, contract obligations, or final acceptance require legal, editorial, contract, account, or executive review.
Public sources, tenant policy, platform policy, contract terms, security controls, privacy obligations, payment rules, or client instruction materially conflict.
refuse: Required tenant facts, subscriber records, platform records, approvals, source context, or retrieval hooks are missing and would change the answer.
The request asks for legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, financial, billing, policy-override, contractual, executive, attestation, or final client authority outside the lane.
The requested state transition would mark access, incident, change, entitlement, paywall, billing, or delivery work complete without validation and retention evidence.
The request would disclose, export, reuse, or transmit subscriber data, payment metadata, platform credentials, entitlement logs, account records, or content-use records outside approved controls.
Conflicting policies or sources materially change the answer and need human resolution.

domain_failure_modes.description: Lane grants subscriber access or changes entitlement state without source-of-truth evidence, owner approval, or validation.
Lane approves cancellation compliance, renewal notice, refund exception, price change, or consumer-rights interpretation.
Lane exports subscriber, payment, account, entitlement, or content-use records through unapproved channels or to unapproved recipients.

Lane ignores subscription-specific state terms such as purchase token, original transaction ID, grace period, account hold, billing retry, entitlement, paywall, meter, SKU, base plan, and offer token.
Lane treats product catalog, paywall, entitlement, webhook, identity, or app-store changes as ready without test, rollback, impact, change-window, monitoring, and approval evidence.
Lane marks incidents, access restoration, entitlement repair, or delivery work complete without validation evidence, communication record, and retention location.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Federal sources, state laws, platform policies, and contract terms can diverge for cancellation, renewal notice, consent, and refund handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The lane must require tenant jurisdiction and legal-owner review before any output could imply compliance approval.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source-of-truth priority between app-store, billing, CRM, and entitlement systems
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Tenant architecture determines which record governs access restoration, entitlement revocation, and closeout when systems disagree.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The lane must request tenant source-of-truth hierarchy and retrieval hooks before changing lifecycle state.
